observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the provincial
government, prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the duly
certified list indicating the name of the taxpayers and the amount of CY
2024 RPT/SET due and collectible and submit the same report annually;
and
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary correcting entries to
adjust the RPT/SET receivables in CY 2024, in accordance with the pro-
forma accounting entries prescribed in the NGAS Manual and certified
list of RPT collectibles from the Municipal Treasurer.

2.12 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The RHU submit a copy of all source documents evidencing receipt of
donated drugs medicines in CY 2024 and all subsequent donations
received to the Accounting Office;
b. The Municipal Accountant establish the correct amount of received
drugs and medicines in CY 2024 and prepare the necessary journal
entries to correct the previous journal entries; and
c. The Municipal Accountant record the issuance of inventories based on
the actual distributed items to the requesting unit or third-party
beneficiaries/end-users, if applicable.

3.12 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary accounting entries for
the reclassification of the completed 20% Development Fund projects
amounting to ₱9.016 million to the appropriate PPE accounts;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to record the
unrecognized depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation;
c. The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the
discrepancies noted amounting to ₱27.017 million, ₱12.171 million and
₱498,000.00 between their records/reports to effect necessary correcting
entries in the PPE balances, if applicable;
d. The Municipal Planning and Development Officer submit the status
report of all Barangay Infrastructure Projects as of December 31, 2024
to the Municipal Accountant, as reference for completed projects which
could be reclassified to PPE; and
e. The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development
Officer strictly monitor and regularly update the status of completion of
the 20% Development Fund projects by preparing and submitting a
quarterly status report to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor.

4.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant must agree
on the proper recording in the Cash books and General Ledgers
pertaining to online RPT/SET collection, check deposits for RPT and
SET collected by the provincial government and other peculiar
transactions that affect the Cash-Local Treasury account;
b. The Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
reconcile their records to determine the correct balance of Cash-Local
Treasury as of December 31, 2024;
c. The Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure the reliability of the cash books
by confirming that the daily balance of the cash books tally with the
actual cash on hand/in vault; and
d. The Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
conduct regular reconciliation of their records by preparing a
reconciliation statement at least every end of each quarter, any items
for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each month/quarter. Any discrepancies requiring adjustment should be promptly addressed and resolved to ensure accurate presentation of Cash in Bank balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to promptly submit the LDRRMP and Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to ensure compliance and transparency in the management of disaster risk reduction efforts pursuant to Item 6.2 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide annual appropriations for Gender and Development programs, projects and activities in the Municipality's Annual Budget amounting to at least five percent of the total budget appropriation and that the GAD Focal Person use the GAD Plan and Budget in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant review financial transactions thoroughly and stop the grant of donations and financial assistance without legal basis in adherence to Sections 4 (1) and (7) of P.D. 1445 and the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 in all its financial transactions.

1. The Inventory Committee undertake complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and render a report thereon in accordance with the procedural guidelines under COA Circular No. 2020-006;
2. The Accounting and Property Units undertake collaborative procedures to reconcile all PPEs after both offices updated their records.

We recommended that the required supporting documents in the granting and liquidation of fund transfers to NGOs/POs be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for post-audit to ascertain the completeness and legality of the transactions in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality maximize the GAD Fund by fully implementing programs, projects and activities in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately unliquidated cash advances, especially those remained outstanding for more than 1 year to five years, otherwise, refund the amount advanced to avoid the institution of administrative and criminal charges against the erring officials; We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all cash advances to Officers and Employees are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and do not allow additional cash advance to any official or employee who have unliquidated cash advances unless settlement or proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended that the Municipality of Binalbagan through its BAC Secretariat, strictly comply with the requirement on the submission of copy of perfected contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from issuance thereof together with the supporting documents for review and evaluation process in compliance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee undertake complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and render a report thereon in accordance with the procedural guidelines under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We also recommended that the Accounting and Property Units undertake collaborative procedures to reconcile all PPEs after both offices updated their records.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and issue PAR to establish property accountability of the end-users.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer furnish the Municipal Accountant with a detailed list of allocated vaccines with cost received by the Municipality for appropriate recognition and recording in the books.
For donations received without costs, the Municipal Accountant shall recognize the donations at their fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended that the Management thru the CSWD, LDRRM, and CHO Offices, submit the original copies of the distribution lists of goods and supplies or list of identified barangay or quarantine facility as a basis for confirmation and validation of the funds disbursed.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the BAC update or revise their APP and post it on the GPPB online portal. Also, require the BAC to post the NOA or POs to the GPPB website as required by the GPPB Circular.
We further recommended that the BAC or the Accounting Office furnish the Audit Team copies of the lacking documentary requirements to ensure that the accredited suppliers strictly comply with the requirements of the GPPB Guidelines for Emergency Procurement.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer adhere to the proper requisition procedures in the purchase of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 116 and Section 122 of the, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, and the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims against government funds are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445.
We also recommended that complete supporting documents that are properly accomplished be attached to the disbursement vouchers in adherence to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 9.2 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipality maximize the GAD Fund by fully implementing programs, projects and activities in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer thru the Municipal Accountant regularly submit the Report of Checks Issued and ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically in accordance with existing regulations.
We further recommended that the person responsible for habitual errors be more prudent and responsible in the discharge of their duties and responsibilities to avoid numerous wastage of checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal LDRRMO and/or the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare a monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team within the prescribed period and in the prescribed format for assessment and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately unliquidated cash advances, especially those remained outstanding for more than 1 year to five years, otherwise, refund the amount advanced to avoid the institution of administrative and criminal charges against the erring officials.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all cash advances to Officers and Employees are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and do not allow additional cash advance to any official or employee who have unliquidated cash advances unless settlement or proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all its Property, Plant, and Equipment, and to reconcile the results of the physical inventory with the accounting records to ascertain the accuracy and existence of the reported balances in the financial statements in compliance to Section 124, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant hasten the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the depository accounts of the Municipality in order to determine the correctness of the balances of the Cash-in-Bank Accounts appearing in the financial statements. The Accountant should submit the statements to the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that the City Accountant revert the accounts payable totaling P83,852.18 which have been outstanding for more than two (2) years as prescribed under the DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 99-6 dated November 13, 1999, to fairly present the Accounts Payable in the financial statements at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipality observe proper utilization of the 20% Development Fund by adhering to Sec. 3.0 and 4.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to avoid defeating the purpose for which the fund was created. We also recommend that the Municipality should only utilize the fund for projects and programs that are capital expenditure in nature that can contribute to the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant reconcile their records to correct the discrepancy of P328,234.20 in the trust fund book.

Henceforth, regular quarterly reconciliation required under Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 should be religiously followed to avoid existence or accumulation of discrepancies and ensure timely correction of errors if needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should direct  the
   Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of  all
   its Property, Plant, and Equipment, and to reconcile the results  of  the
   physical count with the accounting records to ascertain  the  correctness
   and existence of the reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that management strictly ensure that an employee’s  net
   take home pay do not fall below the minimum required amount  as  provided
   in R.A. No. 10717 in order of preference stated therein. For FY 2017,  we
   recommend that the provisions of Section 47 of  R.A.  10924  be  strictly
   adhered to.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor  must  secure  prior  approval  or
   authorization     from     the     local     Sanggunian     for     those
   projects/programs/activities described in generic terms or for all  those
   lump-sum appropriation pursuant to Supreme Court En  Banc  Decision  G.R.
   No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008 and reiterated in  COA  Memorandum  No.
   2010-014 dated April 22, 2010.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  prepares  the  necessary
   adjusting entries to record the collections from operations  of  cemetery
   to its proper  account  and  succeeding  collections  thereto  should  be
   recorded properly.


      We also recommended that Municipality of Binalbagan exercise  prudence
   in disposition or disbursement of government funds and to comply strictly
   with Section 4 of PD 1445 and Section 22 of Republic Act 7160.

We  recommended  that  Municipality  of  Binalbagan  through  its  BAC
   Secretariat strictly comply with the requirement  on  the  submission  of
   copy of perfected contracts and purchase orders within five  (5)  working
   days upon approval together with the supporting documents for review  and
   evaluation process in compliance with COA Circular  2012-001  dated  June
   14, 2012 and Section 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No.  2009-001  dated
   February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and  Accountant  reconcile
   their records to correct the discrepancy of Php90,000.00 in  the  general
   fund book.

            Henceforth,  regular  quarterly  reconciliation  required  under
   Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 should  be
   religiously followed to avoid existence or accumulation of  discrepancies
   and ensure timely correction of errors if needed.

We recommended that the Municipality  of  Binalbagan  strictly  comply
   with the guidelines  on  the  entitlement,  release,  use,  reporting  of
   Confidential and /or Intelligence Funds pursuant to COA-DBM-DILG-GCGOCCs-
   DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015. Responsibilities of
   the Agency Head, Special Disbursing Officer and the Agency Accountant  as
   indicated in Section 6.3 of the Circular must be strictly observed.

We recommended that the  Municipality  implement  the  project/program
   funded by other government agencies as  required  in  the  Memorandum  of
   Agreement (MOA) so that the purposes for which the fund was  granted  are
   achieved thus, benefitting its constituents.




      Likewise,  a  corresponding  entry  be  made  to  record   the   asset
   representing  the  completed  project  totaling   P983,925.50   and   the
   corresponding depreciation should be recognized.

We recommended that the cash advances involving large amounts  should
   be granted to accountable officers with higher approved amount  of  bond,
   thus higher maximum  cash  accountability,  or  increase  their  approved
   amount of bond in accordance with Section 305 (f) of R.A.  7160,  Section
   7.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and Section I  of
   Annex C of Treasury Circular No. 02 – 009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that Accountable Officers  should  submit   complete
   supporting  documents  to  ascertain  the   occurrence,   accuracy,   and
   completeness of the transactions in compliance to Section 4 (6)  of  P.D.
   1445 and Sections 1.2.1 and 1.2.3 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14,
   2012.


     We also recommended that cash advances for official  travel  should  be
   authorized by the head of the agency, in this case, the Municipal  Mayor,
   in accordance to Section 4 (5) of P.D. 1445, Section 3 of Executive Order
   No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 and Section 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular 2012-001
   dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the concerned officials  and  employees  liquidate
   and/or refund immediately their cash advances pursuant to the  provisions
   of Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10,  1997,
   and COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, to avoid  accumulation
   of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair  presentation  of  the
   relevant accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Government:

     a) Implement strictly the provisions of RA 9003 and intensify the  “no
        segregation,  no  collection  policy”  among  residents  of   every
        barangay.  Further, direct all barangay and barangay  officials  to
        coordinate closely with the Solid Waste Management  Board  on  this
        policy.

     b) Establish a regular collection schedule to support the  segregation
        at source activities

     c) Sustain the monitoring and enforcement activities.

     d) Implement the closure of the  open  dump  site  as  mandated  under
        Section 41 of RA 9003 and the possibility of converting the present
        dump  site  into  a  sanitary  landfill  and/or  converting  a  new
        technological approach to solid waste management where  wastes  are
        better managed, controlled and contained.


     e) Direct the SWMB to ensure that all barangays  in  the  Municipality
        establish Material Recovery Facilities pursuant to Section 32 of RA
        9003.

     f) Exercise strong political will to implement the environmental  laws
        on preserving and protecting the environment  as  mandated  by  the
        Act.

1. We recommended that management strictly adhere to Section 4 (6) of  PD

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the remaining PDAF be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund through the account of the Bureau of Treasury pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013.
observations.managementAction: To revert the unappropriated surplus to the General Fund.
observations.reason: The amount of P1,541.00 remains unremitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive creates an inventory committee to conduct the physical inventory taking of all its Property, Plant and Equipment and shall prepare an inventory report thereon, pursuant to Section 490 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Vol. 1 and Section 57 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume II. We further recommend that the inventory report prepared by the inventory committee shall be reconciled with the records of the Accounting Department before the same shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. In the reconciliation of the RPCI and RPCPPE with the accounting records, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the necessary entries on discrepancies noted to ensure that Inventories and PPE are fairly presented in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Always remind the Inventory Committee before June and December of each year respectively.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee are so slow in conducting the inventory as at middle and end of year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that drivers shall be required duly approved driver's trip tickets before usage of government vehicles at all times. We further recommend that preparation of the Monthly Official Travels prepared by the driver/s of each motor vehicle and based thereon prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing the type of vehicles, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle so that the use of the LGU’s motor vehicle can be properly monitored/controlled and regulated. The same shall be submitted to the auditor as required in the regulations to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.managementAction: Continue to remind the Municipal Administrator not to approved Request for gasoline without the corresponding trip ticket.
observations.reason: Not all trips were supported by trip tickets. Request for gasoline must not be approved without trip tickets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, should closely monitor the liquidation of the cash advances amounting to P 99,488.00 and should withhold the salaries of the concerned accountable officers to ensure full settlement. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher debiting Advances to Officers and Employees (148) and crediting Due from Officers and Employees to reclassify the outstanding cash advances in the Special Education Fund amounting to P 235,116.04.
observations.managementAction: To closely monitor the liquidation of Cash Advances.
observations.reason: Liquidation of CA was not closely monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: The Municipal Mayor shall create an Inventory Committee. The Inventory Committee shall conduct the physical count of the municipal’s inventories and property, plant and equipment, prepare and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE. The results thereon shall be reconciled with the records of the Accounting Department before the same shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. In the reconciliation of the RPCI and RPCPPE with the accounting records, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the necessary entries on discrepancies noted to ensure that Inventories and PPE are fairly presented in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Always remind the Inventory Committee before June and December of each year respectively. (Note: duplicated earlier).
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee are so slow in conducting the inventory as at middle and end of year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make representation with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer that the corresponding disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of the 158 checks issued totaling P9,025,945.33 shall be immediately submitted to her Office which in turn shall be submitted for audit in compliance to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid possible suspension or disallowance of the said transactions. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer draw checks only on duly approved disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC, Head of the Procuring Entity and other concerned LGU officials and personnel should procure supplies, materials and services thru public bidding as the general mode and should resort only to the alternative modes of procurement in highly exceptional cases. Further, the BAC should include in the APP the provisions for foreseeable emergencies and should cause the preparation of updated PMPPs and consolidated APP.
observations.managementAction: Conduct meetings and give reminder letters.
observations.reason: End Users must anticipate their programs/projects and activities so that there will be enough time to conduct public bidding.

Further, funds equivalent to at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations shall be allocated for the implementation thereof.
